events.description: The Consensus Group met to revise estimates for FY 2026 and make its initial forecast for FY 2027.
Kansas long-term decline in oil and natural gas production
Kansas Legislative Research Department report published
discuss estimates and come to a consensus for FY 2026 and FY 2027
net transfers out of the SGF is $298.4 million

evidence.quote: Estimates for the State General Fund (SGF) are developed using a consensus process that involves the Legislative Research Department, Division of the Budget, Department of Revenue, and consulting economists from state universities.
The overall estimate for FY 2026 was increased by $165.9 million.
The long-term decline in oil and natural gas production by the Kansas energy sector is expected to continue during the forecast period.
The estimates include the effects of previously enacted state and federal legislation.
The estimate for net transfers out of the SGF is $298.4 million in FY 2026, an increase in estimated transfers out of $12.4 million from the previous estimate.
Accuracy of Consensus Revenue Estimates For 51 years, SGF revenue estimates for Kansas have been developed using the consensus revenue estimating process.

uncertainties: trade war effects on commodity prices
inconsistent timing of implementation of new withholding tables by employers
ongoing uncertainty about tariffs and trade policy
recommended_next_queries: State General Fund Revenue Estimates for FY 2026 and FY 2027
